texto: Turn and face the Light, and all you see is Brightness. Turn and face Shadow, and all of Life will appear before you.
explicacao: Citação de Stan Sudan sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “Turn and face the Light, and all you see is Brightness. Turn and face Shadow, and all of Life wil…” articula vida, darkness, light em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como reflexao-noturna e vida.

frase_original: Turn and face the Light, and all you see is Brightness. Turn and face Shadow, and all of Life will appear before you.

texto: The secret of wisdom is to be overly curious, like a cat--to take the time to look closely, to use all your senses to see and to touch and to taste and to smell and to hear, and to die because you were overly curious. To keep on wondering...about the afterlife. That is our only mission. Only one. If we don’t wonder, then we will never, ever be curious. Then, if like the cat, you wondered too far...you simply no longer will have to ever wonder again. Your mission will be fulfilled. All because of curiosity.--from The Wise-ass Man who butchered Eve Merriam’s wisdom...and Her Secret
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Stan Sudan: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “The secret of wisdom is to be overly curious, like a cat--to take the time to look closely, to us…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sabedoria, cat-wisdom, curiosity pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de curiosity e life-s-mission.

texto: When the filly, the maiden, the boy and the warrior are ready to take up their Destinies, each will know it. The filly will become a mare, the maiden a woman, the boy a man. The Warrior, however, will pick up a stick and walk from his mother, his people, and his land, and never once remove his eyes from the true red road that lies beneath his feet. And yet, he will never know that he is on his Path. (From Brothers of Light)
